## [00:00 - 03:02]

So one of the requested things that came through on the fat framework was how can we automate getting our data into the offline conversions API. So you've got two ways of getting your offline data in. You can upload it manually through CSV, but who has time for that? Or you can use automation. There are many different ways. Zapier is one of them, Integromat and you know, just coding it yourself and probably many others as well. Many of the back end platforms, the sales databases will all be able to do that. But I'm using Zapier here, A, because it's really cool. I can create a zap and I can share that with you. So the link is below this video for you to import the zap that I'm going to show you. I'm going to show you how to take data from Shopify and push that into your Facebook data. So what I've done is to pre create this because it is a bit fiddly and time consuming. So I'm going to show you the fat template. I'm going to show you how to implement the Shopify sales data into your conversion API into your offline conversion API. And then I'll also show you how this can actually work for pretty much any platform that is connected to Zapier. Because all I'm doing here is pulling out data from a sales database. So my goal here is to say if I've had a purchase, push it into the cappy, the Facebook conversion API, and at the same time, put it through to offline conversions. Now you're probably going to argue why do you need both? You don't, you know, by default, Shopify is doing the job of cappy. And that's usually good enough. But the thing about offline conversions is if the conversion happens and it's outside of the tracking window, then Facebook will record it. So let me just give you a scenario. User clicks and add, they end up buying 10 days later, you send the purchase event to the first Facebook cappy, your ad and ad set and campaign, it won't show the conversion because it's outside of the seven day tracking period, you send it to offline conversions. And it does appear because offline conversions currently goes up to 28 days. So this is the reason why it is not a straight duplication to say that, you know, the data's in cappy. So what's the point in putting offline? So I hope you understand that. So this is what I've got set up. And again, the link will be below for you to literally copy and paste this into your Zapier account. And even if you don't run it on Zapier, that's absolutely fine. You can still put it into Zapier, open a new account, or go into your existing account and see how it's working so that you can replicate it in any other tool as well.
